members.name: GetAabb
members.summary: Returns the visibility axis-aligned bounding box in local space.

members.name: GetInstanceColor
members.summary: Gets a specific instance's color multiplier.

members.name: GetInstanceCustomData
members.summary: Returns the custom data that has been set for a specific instance.

members.name: GetInstanceTransform
members.summary: Returns the [Transform3D] of a specific instance.

members.name: GetInstanceTransform2d
members.summary: Returns the [Transform2D] of a specific instance.

members.name: ResetInstancePhysicsInterpolation
members.summary: When using [i]physics interpolation[/i], this function allows you to prevent interpolation on an instance in the current physics tick. This allows you to move instances instantaneously, and should usually be used when initially placing an instance such as a bullet to prevent graphical glitches.

members.name: ResetInstancesPhysicsInterpolation
members.summary: When using [i]physics interpolation[/i], this function allows you to prevent interpolation for all instances in the current physics tick. This allows you to move all instances instantaneously, and should usually be used when initially placing instances to prevent graphical glitches.

members.name: SetBufferInterpolated
members.signature: public void SetBufferInterpolated(PackedFloat32Array bufferCurr, PackedFloat32Array bufferPrev)
members.summary: An alternative to setting the [member buffer] property, which can be used with [i]physics interpolation[/i]. This method takes two arrays, and can set the data for the current and previous tick in one go. The renderer will automatically interpolate the data at each frame. This is useful for situations where the order of instances may change from physics tick to tick, such as particle systems. When the order of instances is coherent, the simpler alternative of setting [member buffer] can still be used with interpolation.

members.name: SetInstanceColor
members.signature: public void SetInstanceColor(int instance, Color color)
members.summary: Sets the color of a specific instance by [i]multiplying[/i] the mesh's existing vertex colors. This allows for different color tinting per instance. [b]Note:[/b] Each component is stored in 32 bits in the Forward+ and Mobile rendering methods, but is packed into 16 bits in the Compatibility rendering method. For the color to take effect, ensure that [member use_colors] is [code]true[/code] on the [MultiMesh] and [member BaseMaterial3D.vertex_color_use_as_albedo] is [code]true[/code] on the material. If you intend to set an absolute color instead of tinting, make sure the material's albedo color is set to pure white ([code]Color(1, 1, 1)[/code]).

members.name: SetInstanceCustomData
members.signature: public void SetInstanceCustomData(int instance, Color customData)
members.summary: Sets custom data for a specific instance. [param custom_data] is a [Color] type only to contain 4 floating-point numbers. [b]Note:[/b] Each number is stored in 32 bits in the Forward+ and Mobile rendering methods, but is packed into 16 bits in the Compatibility rendering method. For the custom data to be used, ensure that [member use_custom_data] is [code]true[/code]. This custom instance data has to be manually accessed in your custom shader using [code]INSTANCE_CUSTOM[/code].

members.name: SetInstanceTransform
members.signature: public void SetInstanceTransform(int instance, Transform3D transform)
members.summary: Sets the [Transform3D] for a specific instance.

members.name: SetInstanceTransform2d
members.signature: public void SetInstanceTransform2d(int instance, Transform2D transform)
members.summary: Sets the [Transform2D] for a specific instance.

members.name: ColorArray
members.summary: Array containing each [Color] used by all instances of this mesh.

members.name: CustomAabb
members.summary: Custom AABB for this MultiMesh resource. Setting this manually prevents costly runtime AABB recalculations.

members.name: CustomDataArray
members.summary: Array containing each custom data value used by all instances of this mesh, as a [PackedColorArray].

members.name: InstanceCount
members.summary: Number of instances that will get drawn. This clears and (re)sizes the buffers. Setting data format or flags afterwards will have no effect. By default, all instances are drawn but you can limit this with [member visible_instance_count].

members.name: Mesh
members.summary: [Mesh] resource to be instanced. The looks of the individual instances can be modified using [method set_instance_color] and [method set_instance_custom_data].

members.name: PhysicsInterpolationQuality
members.summary: Choose whether to use an interpolation method that favors speed or quality. When using low physics tick rates (typically below 20) or high rates of object rotation, you may get better results from the high quality setting. [b]Note:[/b] Fast quality does not equate to low quality. Except in the special cases mentioned above, the quality should be comparable to high quality.

members.name: Transform2dArray
members.summary: Array containing each [Transform2D] value used by all instances of this mesh, as a [PackedVector2Array]. Each transform is divided into 3 [Vector2] values corresponding to the transforms' [code]x[/code], [code]y[/code], and [code]origin[/code].

members.name: TransformArray
members.summary: Array containing each [Transform3D] value used by all instances of this mesh, as a [PackedVector3Array]. Each transform is divided into 4 [Vector3] values corresponding to the transforms' [code]x[/code], [code]y[/code], [code]z[/code], and [code]origin[/code].

members.name: TransformFormat
members.summary: Format of transform used to transform mesh, either 2D or 3D.

members.name: UseColors
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [MultiMesh] will use color data (see [method set_instance_color]). Can only be set when [member instance_count] is [code]0[/code] or less. This means that you need to call this method before setting the instance count, or temporarily reset it to [code]0[/code].

members.name: UseCustomData
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [MultiMesh] will use custom data (see [method set_instance_custom_data]). Can only be set when [member instance_count] is [code]0[/code] or less. This means that you need to call this method before setting the instance count, or temporarily reset it to [code]0[/code].

members.name: VisibleInstanceCount
members.summary: Limits the number of instances drawn, -1 draws all instances. Changing this does not change the sizes of the buffers.

values.name: INTERP_QUALITY_FAST
values.summary: Always interpolate using Basis lerping, which can produce warping artifacts in some situations.

values.name: INTERP_QUALITY_HIGH
values.summary: Attempt to interpolate using Basis slerping (spherical linear interpolation) where possible, otherwise fall back to lerping.

constants.name: INTERP_QUALITY_FAST
constants.summary: Always interpolate using Basis lerping, which can produce warping artifacts in some situations.

constants.name: INTERP_QUALITY_HIGH
constants.summary: Attempt to interpolate using Basis slerping (spherical linear interpolation) where possible, otherwise fall back to lerping.
